questions: What are the types of IP?
answers: Intellectual property (IP) refers to creations of the mind — inventions, designs, brand names, artistic works — that are legally protected from unauthorized use.
Intellectual property holo moner srishti — abishkar, design, brand name, shilpokormo — jegulo ain diyey rokkha kora hoy.
The main types of IP are patents (inventions), copyrights (creative works), trademarks (brand identifiers), and trade secrets (confidential business info).
IP protection is crucial because it incentivizes innovation, lets creators profit from their work, and prevents others from unfairly copying or stealing original ideas.

feelings.question: intellectual property ki?
feelings.answer: Answer: Intellectual property refers to the legal protection that creators and innovators receive for their work, including inventions, designs, brand names, artistic works, and more. It is a crucial tool for encouraging innovation, protecting intellectual property rights, and preventing unfair copying or stealing of original ideas.
